en: Paul, a servant of Christ Jesus, called to be an apostle, set apart for the gospel of God,
zh: 基督耶穌的僕人保羅，蒙召作使徒，奉派傳 神的福音。

en: which he promised beforehand through his prophets in the holy Scriptures,
zh: 這福音是 神藉著眾先知在聖經上預先所應許的，

en: concerning his Son, who was descended from David according to the flesh
zh: 就是論到他的兒子我們的主耶穌基督：按肉身說，他是從大衛的後裔生的；

en: and was declared to be the Son of God in power according to the Spirit of holiness by his resurrection from the dead, Jesus Christ our Lord,
zh: 按聖潔的靈說，因為從死人中復活，顯明他是大有能力的、 神的兒子 。

en: through whom we have received grace and apostleship to bring about the obedience of faith for the sake of his name among all the nations,
zh: 我們從他領受了恩典和使徒的職分，在萬族中使人因他的名相信而順服，

en: including you who are called to belong to Jesus Christ,
zh: 其中也有你們這蒙耶穌基督所召的人。

en: To all those in Rome who are loved by God and called to be saints: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
zh: 我寫信給各位住在羅馬，為 神所愛，蒙召為聖徒的人。願恩惠平安從我們的父 神和主耶穌基督臨到你們。

en: First, I thank my God through Jesus Christ for all of you, because your faith is proclaimed in all the world.
zh: 首先，我靠著耶穌基督，為你們大家感謝我的 神，因為你們的信心傳遍天下。

en: For God is my witness, whom I serve with my spirit in the gospel of his Son, that without ceasing I mention you
zh: 我在傳揚他兒子福音的事上，用心靈事奉的 神，可以作證我是怎樣不斷地記念你們，

en: always in my prayers, asking that somehow by God's will I may now at last succeed in coming to you.
zh: 常常在禱告中懇切祈求，也許我可以照著他的旨意，終於能夠順利地到你們那裡去。

en: For I long to see you, that I may impart to you some spiritual gift to strengthen you-
zh: 因為我很想見你們，好把一些屬靈的恩賜分給你們，使你們可以堅定；

en: that is, that we may be mutually encouraged by each other's faith, both yours and mine.
zh: 也使我在你們中間，藉著你我彼此的信心，大家一同得到安慰。

en: I want you to know, brothers, that I have often intended to come to you (but thus far have been prevented), in order that I may reap some harvest among you as well as among the rest of the Gentiles.
zh: 弟兄們，我不願意你們不知道，我好幾次預先定好了要到你們那裡去，為了要在你們中間也得一些果子，像在其他的民族中間一樣，可是直到現在還有阻礙。

en: I am under obligation both to Greeks and to barbarians, both to the wise and to the foolish.
zh: 無論是希臘人或是未開化的人，聰明的人或是愚笨的人，我都欠他們的債。

en: So I am eager to preach the gospel to you also who are in Rome.
zh: 所以，對我來說，我隨時都願意把福音也傳給你們在羅馬的人。

en: For I am not ashamed of the gospel, for it is the power of God for salvation to everyone who believes, to the Jew first and also to the Greek.
zh: 我不以福音為恥；這福音是 神的大能，要救所有相信的，先是猶太人，後是希臘人。

en: For in it the righteousness of God is revealed from faith for faith, as it is written, "The righteous shall live by faith."
zh: 神的義就是藉著這福音顯明出來，本於信而歸於信，正如經上所記：“義人必因信得生。”

en: For the wrath of God is revealed from heaven against all ungodliness and unrighteousness of men, who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth.
zh: 神的震怒，從天上向所有不虔不義的人顯露出來，就是向那些以不義壓制真理的人顯露出來。

en: For what can be known about God is plain to them, because God has shown it to them.
zh: 神的事情，人所能知道的，在他們裡面原是明顯的，因為 神已經向他們顯明了。

en: For his invisible attributes, namely, his eternal power and divine nature, have been clearly perceived, ever since the creation of the world, in the things that have been made. So they are without excuse.
zh: 其實自從創世以來， 神那看不見的事，就如他永恆的大能和神性，都是看得見的，就是從他所造的萬物中可以領悟，叫人沒有辦法推諉。

en: For although they knew God, they did not honor him as God or give thanks to him, but they became futile in their thinking, and their foolish hearts were darkened.
zh: 因為他們雖然知道 神，卻不尊他為 神，也不感謝他，反而心思變為虛妄，愚頑的心就迷糊了。

en: Claiming to be wise, they became fools,
zh: 他們自以為是聰明的，卻成了愚蠢的。

en: and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man and birds and animals and reptiles.
zh: 他們用必朽壞的人、飛禽、走獸和昆蟲的形象，取代了永不朽壞的 神的榮耀。

en: Therefore God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts to impurity, to the dishonoring of their bodies among themselves,
zh: 因此， 神就任憑他們順著心中的私慾去作污穢的事，以致羞辱自己的身體。

en: because they exchanged the truth about God for a lie and worshiped and served the creature rather than the Creator, who is blessed forever! Amen.
zh: 他們用虛謊取代了 神的真理，敬拜事奉受造之物，卻不敬拜事奉造物的主。他是永遠可稱頌的，阿們。

en: For this reason God gave them up to dishonorable passions. For their women exchanged natural relations for those that are contrary to nature;
zh: 因此， 神就任憑他們放縱可恥的情慾：他們的女人把原來的性的功能，變成違反自然的功能；

en: and the men likewise gave up natural relations with women and were consumed with passion for one another, men committing shameless acts with men and receiving in themselves the due penalty for their error.
zh: 同樣地，男人也捨棄了女人原來的性功能，彼此慾火攻心，男人與男人作出可恥的事。他們這樣妄為，就在自己身上受到應該受的報應。

en: And since they did not see fit to acknowledge God, God gave them up to a debased mind to do what ought not to be done.
zh: 他們既然故意不認識 神， 神就任憑他們存著敗壞的心，去作那些不正當的事。

en: They were filled with all manner of unrighteousness, evil, covetousness, malice. They are full of envy, murder, strife, deceit, maliciousness. They are gossips,
zh: 這些人充滿了各樣的不義、邪惡、貪心、陰險；滿懷嫉妒、兇殺、好鬥、欺詐、幸災樂禍；又是好說讒言的、

en: slanderers, haters of God, insolent, haughty, boastful, inventors of evil, disobedient to parents,
zh: 毀謗人的、憎恨 神的、凌辱人的、傲慢的、自誇的、製造惡事的、忤逆父母的、

en: foolish, faithless, heartless, ruthless.
zh: 冥頑不靈的、不守信用的、冷酷無情的、沒有惻隱之心的。

en: Though they know God's decree that those who practice such things deserve to die, they not only do them but give approval to those who practice them.
zh: 他們雖然明明知道行這些事的人， 神判定他們是該死的，然而他們不單自己去行，也喜歡別人去行。
